content: Cardiac rehabilitation: Class I recommendation for all post-MI patients. Supervised program includes exercise training, education, counseling, risk factor modification. Reduces mortality ~20-25%, hospital readmissions, improves functional capacity and quality of life. Underutilized (<30% eligible participate). Components: aerobic exercise (target 30-60 min most days), resistance training, flexibility exercises. Lifestyle modifications: smoking cessation (most impactful—reduces recurrent MI risk by 50%; use counseling, pharmacotherapy [varenicline, bupropion, NRT]), Mediterranean diet (PREDIMED trial showed 30% CV event reduction with Mediterranean diet plus nuts/olive oil vs low-fat diet), weight loss if overweight, stress management, depression screening/treatment (depression common post-MI, associated with worse outcomes), sleep optimization (screen for sleep apnea). Alcohol moderation (≤1 drink/day women, ≤2 men). Sexual activity can resume when able to climb 2 flights of stairs without symptoms (typically 1-2 weeks post-uncomplicated MI). Influenza and pneumococcal vaccination recommended.
